desc: Onomichi is a steep-set port town on the Seto Inland Sea where temple hills, shipyard ferries, and cycling bridges create a distinct rhythm: spring blossoms and mild riding, humid summer island travel, then clear autumn viewpoints.
hls: Temple Walk hillside perspectives over the Seto Inland Sea channel
Classic western gateway to the Shimanami Kaidō cycling route
Retro slope-side neighborhoods, ropeway access, and local ramen culture
Ferry-linked island life that blends urban rail access with coastal scenery

todo.name: Shimanami Kaidō island segment ride
todo.desc: Weather-dependent: best on dry, lower-wind days for bridge crossings and safer descents.

todo.name: Senkōji ropeway + park viewpoints
todo.desc: Weather-dependent: go in clear conditions for inland-sea visibility at dusk.

todo.name: Onomichi City Museum + shopping arcades
todo.desc: All-weather option pairing indoor exhibits with covered arcade streets.

todo.name: Temple Walk route
todo.desc: Mix major hillside temples and old lanes; carry rain gear for stair-heavy sections.

todo.name: Onomichi ramen comparison stop
todo.desc: All-weather option near station/waterfront shops, useful on rainy cycling days.

todo.name: Mukaishima short ferry hop
todo.desc: Quick crossings show working-port neighborhoods without committing to full-cycle loops.

wifi.note: Reliable in station-area hotels and cafés; older ryokan may have weaker Wi-Fi.

prac.fltNote: Most UK/Ireland visitors arrive through Kansai (KIX) or Tokyo (HND/NRT), then transfer by shinkansen to Fukuyama or Mihara plus a local JR leg to Onomichi; Hiroshima Airport (HIJ) works as a regional alternate with airport-limousine bus + rail transfer, and demand tightens around sakura, Golden Week, Obon, and autumn long weekends.
prac.currency: Japan local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Cycling route bridges can be windy—check advisories before committing to long island segments.
Many cafés and galleries close midweek, so avoid packing all key stops into one weekday.
Typhoon season can suspend ferries and ropeway operations with short notice.

sweet: April, May, and late October are usually best for cycling comfort and views, but expect higher flight and room pressure around sakura/Golden Week; June and November are quieter value picks with greater rain or cooler evenings.
